logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotels in Da Lat such as Du Parc Hotel Deluxe; plentiful options in the highlands.

final_verdict.one_liner: Scenic highland road challenge in Vietnam's cool Central Highlands.

videos.title: Last Chain to Da Nang Cycle a Difference Vietnam Challenge Ride 2013
videos.description:
Video of the 2013 Cycle a Difference Vietnam Challenge Ride, which raised money for the first Neonatal Nurse Training programme in Vietnam.

For more information on upcoming rides see www.cycle-a-difference.com. 

For the information on the charity, see www.newbornsvietnam.org

Filmed by David Salas and produced by Unconstrained Films and Media with sponsorship from RCUK (Road Cycling UK).

#phattireventures #mtbvietnam #mtbdalat
tags: things to do in da lat

videos.title: 24/03/2018: Da Lat Challenge 2018 - 46 km - Mountain bikes
videos.description:
On 24 and 25 of March 2018, I went to Da Lat, Vietnam, to participate at the 2018 Da Lat MTB Challenge.  This yearly event was an opportunity to challenge myself and see what I could do after a few months back on a saddle...
Well, Da Lat Challenge 2018 has been a REAL personal challenge in many ways. I did not finish the race, I was way behind schedule but I still gave it all I had for 8 hours :)... I originally gave myself 4 hours to finish the challenge, I was way far from knowing what was awaiting me... I did 38 km out of 45... My hardest ride to date ;)
Overall I had an amazing time, my special thanks to Mr Biker Saigon, the all team was FANTASTIC!!! 
This was definitely an highlight for 2018!!!
Shot in Full HD, 60 fps with a YI4K Sport camera and edited on Sony Vegas, as usual.
Thanks for watching and if you are in Ho Chi Min City (also called Saigon) and want to do the best bicycle tour drop to my friend Mr Biker: http://mrbikersaigon.com/en
Thank you for watching, hope you liked it, if you did, a little click on the like button maybe? Cheers!
videos.curation_reason: First-person race recap of the actual Da Lat Challenge event with specific race experience details.

key_challenges.name: 1.5 km uphill climb
key_challenges.description: Very challenging 1.5 kilometer uphill section that leaves riders out of breath. One rider described being quite tired and very out of breath after this climb.

terrain_notes.text: The route gets very slippery when wet. Riders report tires having almost no traction, making wet conditions particularly challenging.

gear_mentions.text: Disc brakes are very important for the route, especially described as very sharp and necessary for safe descending.

race_day_tips.text: The group finished significantly ahead of schedule, completing 65km in much less time than expected, so riders should be prepared for potentially faster pacing than anticipated.

additional_quotes.text: We have a crew of six persons and we are quite ready to go so let's see if I can get some footage from the actual run but yeah it's really beautiful out here and it's going to be a great run. It's now 9:00 in the morning and we're going to cycle until 3:30 or something and have lunch maybe have a swim in between.

rider_intel.search_text: The Vietnam Cycling Challenge in Da Lat features a challenging 1.5 kilometer uphill climb around the 20km mark that leaves riders significantly out of breath. The route includes a major 30km downhill section following the main climb. Terrain becomes very slippery when wet, with riders reporting tires having almost no traction in wet conditions. Cars drive aggressively on the route. The course offers beautiful valley scenery and mountain views with foggy conditions at higher elevations. Riders report the route can be completed faster than expected, with groups finishing well ahead of schedule. Disc brakes are essential equipment for safe descending. The ride starts around 9:00 AM with planned finish around 3:30 PM, including lunch breaks and potential swimming stops. Local people are friendly and cheer for cyclists along the route.
